author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
There is No Incompatibility Between Necessity and Free Will.
title: There is No Incompatibility Between Necessity and Free Will.

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: Chapter 54 [XLVI.]—There is No Incompatibility Between Necessity and Free Will.

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: Now how does all this apply to our subject?

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: Let us see what he makes out of it.

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: “Whatever,” says he, “is fettered by natural necessity is deprived of determination of will and deliberation.”

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: Well, now, here lies a question; for it is the height of absurdity for us to say that it does not belong to our will that we wish to be happy, on the ground that it is absolutely impossible for us to be unwilling to be happy, by reason of some indescribable but amiable coercion of our nature; nor dare we maintain that God has not the will but the necessity of righteousness, because He cannot will to sin.
